title: e2e: kube play, port conflict?
body:
Seen once in October, then all of a sudden twice this week:
```
Podman kube play
  [It] test with reserved PublishAll annotation in yaml
...
EXPOSE (2001-2005)
...
# podman [options] kube play /tmp/podman_test2518800159/pod.yaml
starting container SHA1: cannot listen on the TCP port: listen tcp4 :41457: bind: address already in use
starting container SHA2: a dependency of container SHA2 failed to start: container state improper
Error: failed to start 2 containers
```
root and rootless (root error messages are better). I can't find anything helpful in the journal. I can't find the source of the conflict. Am giving up for now, filing for posterity.

### Podman kube play test with reserved PublishAll annotation in yaml
* fedora-38 : int podman fedora-38 rootless host boltdb
  * PR #17831
    * [01-17 16:17](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5879463490093056/html/int-podman-fedora-38-rootless-host-boltdb.log.html#t--Podman-kube-play-test-with-reserved-PublishAll-annotation-in-yaml--1)
* fedora-39 : int podman fedora-39 root container sqlite
  * PR #17831
    * [01-17 23:01](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4708430456291328/html/int-podman-fedora-39-root-container-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-kube-play-test-with-reserved-PublishAll-annotation-in-yaml--1)
* fedora-39β : int podman fedora-39β rootless host boltdb
  * PR #17831
    * [10-24-2023 16:04](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4684023591600128/html/int-podman-fedora-39β-rootless-host-boltdb.log.html#t--Podman-kube-play-test-with-reserved-PublishAll-annotation-in-yaml--1)

| x | x | x | x | x | x |
| ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |
| int(3) | podman(3) | fedora-39β(1) | rootless(2) | host(2) | boltdb(2) |
|  |  | fedora-39(1) | root(1) | container(1) | sqlite(1) |
|  |  | fedora-38(1) |  |  |  |

EDIT: my hunch is that this is a test bug, not a podman bug.

comments.body:
This is a podman issue not the tests, dup of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/19048 IMO

see https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10205#issuecomment-1010055023 for more reasons why this happens.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
